source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage to Crane/Lifting Device

event.top_event: Loss of Hand Function - Finger Amputation and Crushing Injury
actions_taken: Injured person evacuated via medivac to Crescent City Hospital in New Orleans, Louisiana
Fourth finger amputated down to first knuckle
event.summary: While removing a gearbox coupling and mud agitator shaft for repair on the T.O. Discoverer India, the injured person (IP) and mechanic deviated from the written risk assessment (WRA) procedure. The IP installed support beams out of sequence and attempted to adjust a safety clamp without first securing it properly. While hammering an Allen wrench onto a corroded bolt head on the agitator shaft, the 750-pound shaft and coupling suddenly separated from the gearbox output shaft and fell, crushing the IP's fourth and fifth fingers between the wrench and support beams. The incident was preceded by three prior unexpected separations of the agitator shaft from the gearbox output shaft (2010, 2014, 2015), but no additional precautionary measures were implemented.
recommendations: Update procedures and WRAs to include hazards associated with unexpected coupling failure and separation of agitator shaft from gearbox output shaft
Implement additional precautionary measures to mitigate the hazard of unexpected coupling failure
Enforce company policy requiring all participating members of a job task to individually sign the WRA
Ensure personnel wear appropriate personal protective equipment (impact gloves) during repair tasks

hazards.name: Unexpected separation of agitator shaft from gearbox output shaft due to tapered coupling failure
Heavy suspended load (750-pound agitator shaft and coupling) without adequate restraint

threats.name: Deviation from written risk assessment procedure - out of sequence installation of support beams
Safety clamp not properly secured before work commenced on agitator assembly
Failure to use correct tool size (Allen wrench) as specified in WRA
Hammering on corroded bolt head without proper restraint system in place
Lack of stop work authority enforcement and failure to call timeout for work outside plan
Prior knowledge of coupling failure hazard not communicated or mitigated

consequences.name: Crush injury to left hand - fourth and fifth fingers
Amputation of fourth finger at first knuckle
Severe crushing and fracture of fifth finger
Lost time accident (>3 days) and medical evacuation
Permanent loss of hand function and dexterity

controls.name: Written Risk Assessment (WRA) procedure for gearbox coupling and agitator shaft removal
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure sequential work steps and tool requirements are documented
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to prevent deviation from safe work procedures

supporting_text: PERSONNEL DEVIATED FROM THE SEQUENTIAL JOB STEPS AS OUTLINED IN THE WRA
IP AND CREW MEMBER DETERMINED THE SAFETY CLAMP NEEDED TO BE ADJUSTED TO A SMALLER SIZE AFTER THEY INITIALLY TRIED TO SECURE THE CLAMP AS IDENTIFIED IN STEP 8 IN THE WRA
THEY PERFORMED THIS STEP OUT OF SEQUENCE AS OUTLINED IN THE WRITTEN RISK ASSESSMENT (WRA)

controls.name: Safety clamp (drill collar clamp) to secure agitator shaft before separation from gearbox
controls.barrier_role: Engineering control to prevent unexpected separation and falling load
controls.lod_basis: Primary mechanical restraint to prevent load drop

supporting_text: WITHOUT THE SAFETY CLAMP INSTALLED, THE IP PUT THE ALLEN WRENCH ONTO AN ALLEN BOLT HEAD ON THE AGITATOR SHAFT

IP AND MECHANIC HAD LIFTED GEAR BOX WITH TAPERED COUPLING AND AGITATOR SHAFT ATTACHED IN ORDER TO PLACE SAFETY CLAMP (DRILL COLLAR CLAMP) ON THE AGITATOR SHAFT BEFORE SEPARATING THE AGITATOR SHAFT FROM THE GEARBOX
THE IP WAS WORKING ON THE AGITATOR SHAFT AND COUPLING WITHOUT THE SAFETY CLAMP PROPERLY IN PLACE

controls.name: Support beams with all-thread installed around agitator shaft
controls.barrier_role: Secondary mechanical restraint to catch or limit fall of separated shaft
controls.lod_basis: Secondary containment to limit consequences of unexpected separation

supporting_text: PRIOR TO SECURING SAFETY CLAMP, THE CREW MEMBERS INSTALLED THE SUPPORT BEAMS WITH ALL THREAD AROUND THE AGITATOR SHAFT
THE SUPPORT BEAMS WERE PUT IN PLACE PRIOR TO THE SAFETY CLAMP BEING CORRECTLY SECURED
THE IP'S FOURTH AND FIFTH FINGERS ON HIS LEFT HAND WERE SMASHED AND PINNED UNDER THE WEIGHT OF THE SHAFT BETWEEN THE END OF THE ALLEN WRENCH AND SUPPORT BEAMS INSTALLED BELOW

controls.name: Impact gloves personal protective equipment
controls.barrier_role: PPE to reduce severity of crush injury to hands
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to reduce injury severity

supporting_text: THE IP, AS MENTIONED BY THE OIM, LEAD DRILL SITE MANAGER, AND SENIOR TOOLPUSHER; SHOULD HAD BEEN WEARING HIS IMPACT GLOVES DURING THIS STAGE OF THE REPAIR

controls.name: Stop work authority and timeout procedures
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to halt work when deviations from plan are observed
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative control to prevent work outside approved procedures

supporting_text: THE MECHANIC DID NOT STOP THE IP FROM WORKING ON THE AGITATOR ASSEMBLY WITHOUT THE SAFETY CLAMP IN PLACE, THUS; NOT UTILIZING STOP WORK AUTHORITY AND NOT CALLING FOR A 'TIME OUT' FOR WORKING OUTSIDE OF THE PLAN CREATING A HAZARDOUS WORK ENVIRONMENT LEADING TO AN INJURY OCCURRENCE

controls.name: WRA sign-off requirement - all participating crew members must individually sign
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure all personnel acknowledge and commit to following procedure
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative control to ensure personnel accountability and procedure awareness

supporting_text: CREW MEMBERS NOT FOLLOWING COMPANY POLICY IN REGARDS TO EACH PARTICIPATING MEMBER OF A JOB TASK MUST SIGN THE WRA INDIVIDUALLY
THE MECHANIC PRINTED THE IP'S NAME ON THE WRA. THE IP NEVER SIGNED THE WRA

controls.name: Manufacturer's procedure for agitator shaft and gearbox coupling repair
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control based on equipment manufacturer's specifications
controls.lod_basis: Primary control based on manufacturer's design and safety specifications

supporting_text: FAILURE OF IP FOLLOWING MAUFACTURER'S PROCEDURE IN THE PERFORMANCE OF THE TASK

THE TOOL LIST IN THE WRA IDENTIFIED A 19MM ALLEN WRENCH WAS NEEDED FOR THE JOB TASK. THE IP CHECKED TO SEE IF HIS WRENCH WOULD FIT THE BOLT EVEN THOUGH THE WRA OUTLINED WHAT SIZE TOOLS WOULD BE NEEDED FOR THE JOB

controls.name: Hazard mitigation for known coupling failure risk - prior separations in 2010, 2014, 2015
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to implement precautionary measures based on prior incident history
controls.lod_basis: Primary control to address known and recurring hazard

supporting_text: TRANSOCEAN AND LESSEE HAD PRIOR KNOWLEDGE OF SUDDEN UNEXPECTED SEPARATION OF THE AGITATOR GEARBOX OUTPUT SHAFT FROM AGITATOR SHAFT TAPERED COUPLING. ON THREE DIFFERENT OCCASIONS DATING BACK TO 2010, AGAIN IN 2014, AND AGAIN IN 2015 SUDDEN UNEXPECTED SEPARATION OF THE AGITATOR SHAFT FROM THE OUTPUT SHAFT ON THE GEARBOX OCCURRED

THERE WERE NO ADDITIONAL PRECAUTIONARY MEASURES TAKEN TO MITIGATE THE HAZARD OF UNEXPECTED COUPLING FAILURE CAUSING SEPARATION OF THE AGITATOR SHAFT FROM THE GEARBOX OUTPUT SHAFT WHILE PERSONNEL WERE PERFORMING REPAIRS ON THIS EQUIPMENT
PROCEDURES AND WRA'S WERE NOT UPDATED TO INCLUDE THESE HAZARDS, CAUSING THREAT OF INJURY TO PERSONNEL

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
